title: Lets Watch NGE Together Unofficial Commentary Thread
display_title: Lets Watch NGE Together Unofficial Commentary Thread
wikitext:
[]: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Aug 13, 2017 5:06 pm Hello everybody! After a couple years of sitting in storage at my dad's house, I have finally recovered my Perfect Collection of NGE, plus D&R/EoE. I'm planning to go through the series again (first time in at least 3 years) and I wanted a place to share random thoughts of each episode as I watch them. Things I notice, be it the way the plot develops, random moments/themes that pop out to me, the way shots are framed, and any other commentary I might have. Why keep all the fun for myself though? I invite everybody to use this thread for that purpose and to revisit it for future rewatches. This thread will be a place for ongoing, casual conversation for those currently watching or rewatching the series. Please share any thoughts inspired by your latest viewing while it's still fresh in your mind. Questions or ideas you have may have answers or relevant discussion that can be linked to. Some questions or ideas may not have prior discussion to refer to, and can be potential material for new threads. Any tangents that develop in this thread can be split and further explored in their very own threads. Hopefully this can be a place to fuel new ideas to discuss. I'll watch and take notes on Ep 01 tonight and afterward share them here, complete with screencaps and everything. Please join me and share as well, and it can be as though we watched the series together.

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Sun Aug 13, 2017 5:41 pm Sounds like a great idea. I look forward to any discussions that will come of this

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by imprimatur13 » Sun Aug 13, 2017 6:28 pm This DOES sound like a great idea. I may (NOT) participate, but I'll definitely like to read it. I look forward to it! (Besides, even if FGC is revived, this'll give me a chance to give my thoughts on 1-6, if I get around to it...) Thanks Sachi! (P.S. Once you get to Death, I actually have my own comments in a post already.)

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by Cybermat47 » Sun Aug 13, 2017 6:32 pm

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by Joseki » Sun Aug 13, 2017 8:31 pm Do you plan to have a schedule, maybe one episode every day or every week? I would love to rewatch the TV show and post my random thoughts but I don't think I could do it daily.

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Aug 13, 2017 8:42 pm No schedule in mind currently. I doubt I'd be able to do a daily episode either. After the first one, I should have an idea of how much time I need to set aside for each one. Perhaps it can be a weekly thing, to be in the spirit of the way it originally aired. It's good to let the episode stew inside for a bit before moving on. Rushing it might lead to overlooking things. That way we also have time to discuss the episode with each other before moving on as a group to the next.

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by Joy Evangelion » Sun Aug 13, 2017 8:43 pm This is a pretty cool idea Sachi. I can't take part(my yearly viewing is in early December), but I look forward to seeing what everyone will be writing. Luckily I've got all of the episodes down to memory so maybe I can throw my two cents in here or there.

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by imprimatur13 » Sun Aug 13, 2017 9:05 pm @Sachi I second the idea of a weekly thing. There's just so much material in each episode, you can't hope to cover it all in less time. @Joy Evangelion Yearly viewing in early December? Does EoE coincide with January 1st, by any chance ?

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by The Flying Fortress » Sun Aug 13, 2017 9:19 pm Is not there something like that already, like, Eva commentary Project? , Anyway, I wish I could comment on the episodes in a more informal way, I'm not the type to write theses or make very deep comments, so that seems perfect to me.

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by imprimatur13 » Sun Aug 13, 2017 9:24 pm Well, there is , but the Fan Geek Commentary is currently on hiatus. Stopped at episode 7, IIRC. Anyway, this is a chance to start fresh!

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Aug 13, 2017 9:36 pm I don't wish for this to be a formal commentary project. More a casual commentary thread for anyone to participate in at anytime whenever they rewatch the series. Later on, it might just become a "hey, I watched this episode, and had this, this and this to say about it" thread, without committing to full-blown discussion over each thing noticed. To kick it off, I'm starting with my rewatching of the series.

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by imprimatur13 » Sun Aug 13, 2017 9:46 pm Gotcha.

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Aug 14, 2017 3:22 am Alright, I just finished Episode 01. I took more notes than I thought I would, three written pages. Here's what I've got: Right away we're told it's the year 2015. We see a broken city, partially submerged in water. A state of emergency is being declared. From the condition of the city, it's clear that this may not be the first time this sort of emergency has needed to happen. It's a future not too distant, yet still unfamiliar. We're given our first glimpse at our main characters, Shinji and Misato. They're trying to meet up, and both have each other's photos to go by. We see the photos of each before being introduced to them properly, yet we can get an idea of their personalities. Shinji is formal and conservative, while Misato is flirtatious, purposely drawing attention to her cleavage, lipstick stain being the cherry on top. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by Cybermat47 » Mon Aug 14, 2017 6:03 am Absolutely agree with you about Sachiel being more curious than hostile at first. Anno portrayed Godzilla in the same way.

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by The Flying Fortress » Mon Aug 14, 2017 6:54 am Tip: You can put something to identify each scene as '' Ep01S02 '' so we can organize it more simply, even if it's informal, anyway, here are my comments: So, I do not want to be obvious and talk about Sachiel, I'd like to start with Ritsuko: the image shows no but Ritsuko seems to impose a strange way on Misato here when he gets out of the elevator, then Misato apologizes, so that initially I thought Misato was subordinate to Ritsuko or something. The reenchant scene between Shinji and Gendo is really striking to me, this is a time when I thought this was a different anime than I had ever seen before, it was a sense of surprise that created good expectations for me to be able to Progress in the anime.

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by imprimatur13 » Mon Aug 14, 2017 9:45 am Ok, I intend to keep my serious comments separate from the (semi-)humorous ones by putting the funnies in parentheses. @Sachi Importantly, you're right. The theme of broken communication is set up from the very beginning. Never thought about the shirt being an indication of being dragged against his will. Nice. I also never noticed the "blue/red" color change. I'll have to look for it now . (There are 6 Shinji's in the screens. This is because he represents the masculine, while the feminine is represented by 7.) Gendo really does (read with voice of Sofia Vergara) love him... Aww... I wonder if one of the reasons Gendo was so happy: Maybe Yui hadn't surfaced before this. Maybe her soul was dormant, and he was happy now that she was coming back, 'cause of Shin-chan. He (read with voice of Sofia Vergara) loves her... Aw... Right of course, about the cage. Good catch! @Cybermat47 Does he really? I really should watch that one of these days... @FlyingF I agree with the "scene organization" thing. And your last paragraph is very poignant. Ok, here goes: (Sachiel's first weapon? A penis. Coming out of his arm, but any long thin object will be henceforth referred to as such . This ties in to my interpretation of the Angel attacks on NERV as a rape-metaphor. They want to invade NERV, to forcibly "know" the Lilin/Adam, and they are put off by Misato, who specializes in preventing any overly close relationships from developing. Misato also compares Shamshel explicitly to "the worst kind of man". Ok, the used cigarettes and the pencil-breaking. I think they are (what else?) phallic symbols. The cigarettes are limp, and the pencil is broken. Those symbolize the powerlessness of the JSSDF against the Angels. (You know how a cigarette is sometimes just a cigarette? Not if you're watching Eva, it's not.)) It's worth mentioning that Shinji could also have been looking at the cross, if you look closely at his eyes. You know what I notice here? At the shot with Mitchan and Ritchan's cleavages, Ritsuko's breasts are revealed. Misato's, on the other hand, are completely covered, and they have the cross on top. I think this is very interesting, because you think of Misato as the "freer" one, and Ritsuko as the "cold, business-like" one. So, this imagery kind of hints that they're more than they seem on the surface. In fact, like most Eva characters, their outward personae are the exact opposite of their true selves. Which means that Misato's persona is Ritsuko's true self, and vice versa. That's what I think is going on in this shot. (About those pairs, same could be said about Asuka/Rei.) Yui-sama in between dominating Gendo and dominated Shinji, implies a sort of role, as intermediating the father/son relationship. Here's the true meaning of the shot, to me, anyway: Gendo is above Shinji. He's not in contact with him, it's not that he's *dominating* him, he's just so above him as to be unapproachable. This is reinforced by his speech: He's not speaking forcefully. Just matter-of-factly. Shinji's head is lowered in sadness, because he can't connect to Gendo. Now here's the problem: Because Gendo cannot connect to Shinji, he can't perform the duties of a parent. So, Yui takes care of all that. Hence, why she protects him from falling debris. Again, I think that Yui's soul had been dormant till now, and Gendo was smiling at its awakening.

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by The Flying Fortress » Mon Aug 14, 2017 10:45 am imprimatur13 wrote: Ok, the used cigarettes and the pencil-breaking. I think they are (what else?) phallic symbols. The cigarettes are limp, and the pencil is broken. Those symbolize the powerlessness of the JSSDF against the Angels. (You know how a cigarette is sometimes just a cigarette? Not if you're watching Eva, it's not.)

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Aug 14, 2017 2:34 pm Ah yes. Limp cigarettes. I was trying to make a connection between the ash tray and Nerv HQ, and this helps. The ashtray has the Nerv logo on it, and the ridges make it look like a castle wall. The cigarettes are limp and powerless within their fortress walls, unable to defend themselves from attack.

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by imprimatur13 » Mon Aug 14, 2017 10:32 pm @FF Ok, I'll try to tone it down a bit . (Or perhaps enclose it in the "parentheses of humor") @Sachi Nice! Of course, the "pleb" explanation (i.e. the one that actually makes sense , and is not the product of our over-analysis ) is that they were just smoking to calm their nerves, but this tickles my brain . Thanks!

[]: Re: Let's Watch NGE Together! Unofficial Commentary Thread             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Aug 16, 2017 2:46 am I edited the OP a bit to hopefully clarify my intents with this thread. I also resized the images in my initial post about episode 01. One thing that caught my eye when watching the episode, but I couldn't quite gather my thoughts on before was while Shinji and Misato are first entering Nerv. As Misato's car pulls in, the gate closes and the *RED* emergency light turns on. Immediately after the color change, Misato introduces Nerv to Shinji. This promotes the highly secretive and protective nature of Nerv. Answers kept locked away under the highest security. SPOILER: Show
